---
title: "Run and build TRIALMON"
subtitle: "The R Shiny safety-screening console, from running it to building it yourself"
format:
  html:
    theme: darkly
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
    toc-title: On this page
    embed-resources: true
    highlight-style: github-dark
    code-copy: true
execute:
  eval: false
---

## Run it

```r
install.packages(c("shiny", "bslib", "ggplot2"))   # once
shiny::runApp("sasr_monitoring_wiki/trialmon_shiny")
```

Click a point on the eDISH plot to open a participant's evidence packet.

## The one principle: separate the logic from the UI

The screening rules live in plain functions in `trialmon_shiny/R/logic.R`. The app,
the [visual guide](trialmon-guide.html), the [dashboard](trialmon-dashboard.html),
and the unit tests all call the same functions.

## Step 1 - the screening rules, as plain functions

```r
qtc_sev <- function(qt, dqt)
  if (qt > 500 || dqt > 60) "RED" else if (qt > 480 || dqt > 30) "AMBER"
  else if (qt > 450) "watch" else "GREEN"

score_participants <- function(p) {
  p$hys  <- (p$alt > 3 | p$ast > 3) & p$tbl > 2        # the eDISH AND-gate
  p$qsev <- mapply(qtc_sev, p$qt, p$dqt)               # ICH E14 tiers
  p
}
```

The **AND-gate** is the whole point: a high ALT alone does not fire; bilirubin must
be up too. CP101-009 (ALT 4.2x, TBili 1.6x) stays green - a test that would.

## Step 2 - the data is a safety export you can swap

`R/gen_data.R` writes `participants.csv`, `aes.csv`, `cohorts.csv` (values are xULN
multiples, not measurements). On a real study you replace them with a **read-only**
export from the validated safety database / ADaM (ADLB, ADEG, ADAE), same columns.
Nothing writes back.

```r
tm <- trialmon_load("data")
trialmon_status(tm)          # worst of the eDISH / QTcF / 3+3 screens
trialmon_worklist(tm)        # every firing position, ranked RED-first
```

## Step 3 - the signature eDISH scatter

```r
output$edish <- renderPlot({
  ggplot(P, aes(alt, tbl, colour = flag)) +
    annotate("rect", xmin=3, xmax=Inf, ymin=2, ymax=Inf, fill="#e8657a", alpha=.08) +
    geom_hline(yintercept=2, linetype=2) + geom_vline(xintercept=3, linetype=2) +
    geom_point() + scale_x_log10() + scale_y_log10()
})
observeEvent(input$edish_click, {                        # click -> participant
  np <- nearPoints(P, input$edish_click, xvar="alt", yvar="tbl", maxpoints=1)
  if (nrow(np)) updateSelectInput(session, "pid", selected = np$id[1])
})
```

`nearPoints()` maps the click on the log-log plot back to the nearest participant,
which opens the evidence packet.

## Step 4 - the worklist, the 3+3 ladder, the packet

`trialmon_worklist()` builds the ranked flag list; `dlt_state()` rolls DLTs up per
cohort for the 3+3 ladder; the packet reads the selected participant's screens, AEs,
and disposition. All from `R/logic.R`.

## Verify it

```r
Rscript sasr_monitoring_wiki/trialmon_tests/test-logic.R
# checks the AND-gate: CP101-009 (ALT>3, TBili<2) is NOT a Hy's position
```

## Make it yours

- **Change a threshold:** edit `qtc_sev()` or the eDISH gate in `R/logic.R`.
- **Change the data:** swap the CSVs for a safety-DB / ADaM export.
- **Add a screen:** add a rule and a `trialmon_worklist()` entry.

Same skeleton as the [lifecycle monitor](../../lifecycle_dashboard/quarto/lifecycle-build.html),
the [risk console](../../risk_monitor/quarto/risk-build.html), and the
[knowledge graph](../../knowledge_vault/quarto/kg-build.html).
